_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The AAC or American Athletic conference has rebranded as just 'American' which is both confusing and unnecessary. The AAC has been the top rated G6 conference, with Tulane making the CFP last season in 2025. Three storylines define the 2026 American: (1) Three of the AAC's strongest programs all lost their head coaches to P4 schools; (2) The AAC looks tightly compressed in 2026 with 8 or 9 teams capable of winning the conference championship (3) The bottom of the AAC is both well defined and well below the other schools. Tulane won the AAC in 2025, beating North Texas 34-21 in their home Yulman Stadium.  When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Conference USA tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = AAC title-game favorite, lower tier CFB bowl game contenders ; Tier 2 = title-game contender, lower tier bowl game contenders; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game and bowl game outsider; Tier 4 = bottom of the conference where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be a tremendous achievement. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. The American Championship game is played in the home stadium of the team with the best overall conference win percentage.

team_notes.army: Tier 2-3 AAC team; Army does not accept transfers due to military requirements, but has still fielded a consistent winning football team.
team_notes.charlotte: Tier 4 AAC team; Charlotte has very little funding or noticable interest from its own administration to be good at football.
team_notes.memphis: Tier 1 AAC team; Lost HC Ryan Silverfield to Arkansas; New HC Charles Huff hired from Southern Miss, brought 15 players from Southern Miss via the transfer portal.  QB portal additions Marcus Stokes from FCS West Florida is battling Air Noland from South Carolina for QB1 - treat as unsettled.  RB Jaylin Carter played just over 150 snaps last year at Southern Miss, other portal adds will fight for RB1.  WR is a mix of portals, TE Hunter Tipton signed from Middle Tennessee is a big, productive target.  OL has good potential with 7 players with starting experience, all portal additions, three from FCS. DE J'Mond Tapp from Southern Miss is a legit pass rush threat; Secondary a real strength with proven starters like CBs like Anthony Richard Jr. from Southern Miss and Darius Malcolm Jr. from FCS Wofford and safety Ian Foster from Southern Miss. Still an AAC favorite even with all the transition.
team_notes.navy: Tier 2-3 AAC team; Navy does not accept transfers due to military requirements, but has still fielded a consistent winning football team.
team_notes.rice: Tier 4 AAC team; Rice is a high academic institution with real limitations on portal additions needing to meet their academic standards.
